package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_handler_uses_global_singletons.
// Rule intent: Handlers should receive dependencies (DB, cache, config, logger) via
// constructor injection, not reach for global variables.
// Anti-pattern: Handler accesses GlobalDB, GlobalConfig, GlobalCache, etc.

var (
	GlobalDB     *gorm.DB
	GlobalConfig map[string]string
	GlobalCache  *Cache
	GlobalLogger *Logger
)

type UserHandler struct{}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")

	// Anti-pattern: using global DB singleton instead of injected dependency
	var user User
	GlobalDB.Where("id = ?", id).First(&user)

	// Anti-pattern: using global cache singleton
	cached, found := GlobalCache.Get("user:" + id)
	if found {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, cached)
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: using global config singleton
	logLevel := GlobalConfig["log_level"]
	GlobalLogger.Info("fetching user", "id", id, "level", logLevel)

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"user": user})
}

func (h *UserHandler) CreateUser(c *gin.Context) {
	var req struct {
		Name string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
	}
	if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&req); err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": "invalid request"})
		return
	}

	// Anti-pattern: using global DB singleton
	user := User{Name: req.Name}
	GlobalDB.Create(&user)

	// Anti-pattern: using global config singleton
	if GlobalConfig["feature_flags.new_user_welcome"] == "true" {
		GlobalLogger.Info("sending welcome email", "user", user.Name)
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"user": user})
}

type User struct {
	ID   uint   `gorm:"column:id;primaryKey"`
	Name string `gorm:"column:name"`
}

type Cache struct{}

func (c *Cache) Get(key string) (interface{}, bool) {
	return nil, false
}

type Logger struct{}

func (l *Logger) Info(msg string, args ...interface{}) {}
